text: In this video, we would like to present lift procedure for transphacteric posterior anal fistula. A sixty-four years old gentleman presented at our clinic complaining of constant secretion through opening posterior from the anus. According to Park's classification, fistula in our case is uncomplicated transsphincteric fistula.

The patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position with buttocks taped aside. A proctoscope is inserted, and a water test is performed to confirm the presence of an internal opening. The surgeon probes the fistula tract and makes an incision above the intersphincteric groove, identifying the intersphincteric space.

The surgeon identifies and dissects the fistula tract, ligating it with 3-0 micro-interrupted sutures. The tract is then transected and curetted, with the other end also ligated using 3-0 Vicryl interrupted sutures.
The surgeon tightly ligates the fistula tract, then uses electrocautery to cauterize the internal opening of the fistula. The skin defect is closed with three interrupted sutures using Vicryl.
